$comment: Generated by `icm reindex` from each file's <!--icm--> metadata block. Edit the block in the source file, not this file.
name: windows-icm
description: Routing index. Each entry is a reference/pattern/recipe/scaffold/snippet file; the dispatcher routes a question or task to the entry whose summary fits, then grounds on its text. Add an entry here for every file you want routable.
domain: Windows automation and tooling with C# (in-box csc, C# 5) and PowerShell 5.1

entries.title: Building C# with the in-box csc.exe
entries.summary: How to compile with the .NET Framework csc.exe (no SDK/NuGet/MSBuild): invocation, references, exe vs library, and the JSON library to use (JavaScriptSerializer, not System.Text.Json).

entries.title: C# 5 language subset (what the in-box csc allows)
entries.summary: Which modern C# features are NOT available under the in-box csc (langversion 5) - no string interpolation, expression-bodied members, ?., records - and the C# 5 way to write each.

entries.title: Concurrency and async in-box (C# 5 + .NET FW 4.8)
entries.summary: What async/concurrency APIs are available under C# 5 and the in-box framework (Task, async/await, Parallel, BlockingCollection, SemaphoreSlim, Lazy), what is NOT (ValueTask, Channel, Dataflow, IAsyncEnumerable, async Main), and the async rules a model gets wrong.

entries.title: Importable in-box assemblies (no SDK / NuGet)
entries.summary: Which .NET Framework assemblies the in-box csc can reference here, what each unlocks, and the exact -reference: line - plus the high-value ones (WMI, zip, GDI+, WMI) and what is NOT present (WPF, ValueTask, Channels, Dataflow).

entries.title: ICM host conventions (tools, flows, schemas)
entries.summary: How this instance's pieces fit the host: declaring a command/script tool, authoring a flow (incl. the loop node), and the table-schema oracle. Read before adding a tool or flow.

entries.title: PowerShell 5.1 constraints and gotchas
entries.summary: Windows PowerShell 5.1 differences from pwsh 7: no ternary/null-coalescing, no chain operators, here-string rules, UTF-16 default encoding, and other things a modern-trained model gets wrong.

entries.title: Win32 P/Invoke for WinForms polish
entries.summary: P/Invoke signatures the host GUI actually uses (uxtheme SetWindowTheme, shell32 SHGetFileInfo + IFileOpenDialog folder picker, user32 DestroyIcon) plus common additions (dwmapi dark title bar, GetAsyncKeyState) - exact DllImport declarations for a WinForms tool.

entries.title: Chain of Responsibility pattern (pass a request along a chain of handlers)
entries.summary: Decouples a sender from its receivers by giving each handler a chance to process a request or forward it to the next handler in the chain.

entries.title: Command pattern (encapsulate a request as an object)
entries.summary: Wraps a request and its receiver in an object so invokers can parameterize, queue, log, undo, and macro requests without knowing what they do.

entries.title: Iterator pattern (sequential access without exposing the collection)
entries.summary: Provides a uniform way to traverse the elements of an aggregate without exposing how it stores them, so a client can walk an ArrayList and an array through the same interface.

entries.title: Mediator pattern (centralize how a set of objects interact)
entries.summary: Routes communication between colleagues through a single mediator object so the colleagues stay decoupled and the interaction logic lives in one place.

entries.title: Observer pattern (notify dependents when subject state changes)
entries.summary: Defines a one-to-many dependency so that when one subject changes state all its registered observers are notified, here using .NET's IObservable<T>/IObserver<T>.

entries.title: State pattern (object changes behavior when its internal state changes)
entries.summary: Lets an object alter its behavior when its internal state changes by delegating each operation to a separate state object, replacing sprawling conditional logic.

entries.title: Strategy pattern (interchangeable algorithms behind an interface)
entries.summary: Defines a family of algorithms, encapsulates each behind a common interface, and lets the context swap them at runtime via composition instead of inheritance.

entries.title: Template Method pattern (fix an algorithm skeleton, defer steps to subclasses)
entries.summary: Defines the skeleton of an algorithm in a base method while letting subclasses override specific steps without changing the algorithm's structure.

entries.title: Visitor pattern (add operations to an object structure without changing it)
entries.summary: Represents an operation to perform on the elements of an object structure, letting you add new operations without modifying the element classes via double dispatch.

entries.title: Async/await basics (Task-returning methods done right)
entries.summary: The C# 5 async method shape - async Task / async Task<T>, Task.Run for CPU-bound work, Task.WhenAll, awaiting IO - plus the anti-patterns (no async void, never .Result/.Wait() on a UI context, ConfigureAwait(false) in library code).

entries.title: Cooperative cancellation (CancellationToken / CancellationTokenSource)
entries.summary: Cooperatively cancel async and loop work with CancellationToken/CancellationTokenSource: check the token in loops, pass it into Task.Run and Task.Delay, and use new CancellationTokenSource(timeoutMs) for timeouts.

entries.title: Parallel aggregate (fan out over chunks, merge results)
entries.summary: Split a large input into chunks, process them with Parallel.For/ForEach across cores, and merge the per-chunk results with Interlocked counters or a ConcurrentBag<T> - e.g. scanning a big byte[] in parallel.

entries.title: Producer/consumer with BlockingCollection<T> (the in-box channel)
entries.summary: Hand work from producers to consumers with BlockingCollection<T> - the in-box stand-in for Channel<T> (which is NOT available) - using Add/CompleteAdding on the producer side and GetConsumingEnumerable on the consumer side.

entries.title: Throttle concurrency with SemaphoreSlim (bound simultaneous work)
entries.summary: Cap how many operations run at once with SemaphoreSlim(maxConcurrent): await WaitAsync() before the work and Release() in a finally, so you bound simultaneous file/process/network calls.

entries.title: Abstract Factory pattern (create families of related objects)
entries.summary: Provides an interface for creating a whole family of related products without naming their concrete classes, so swapping the factory swaps the entire family.

entries.title: Builder pattern (step-by-step construction of a complex object)
entries.summary: Separates the construction of a complex object from its representation so the same build steps can produce different objects, driven by an optional director.

entries.title: Factory Method pattern (defer product creation to subclasses)
entries.summary: Defines a creator with a fixed algorithm that calls an abstract factory method, letting each subclass decide which concrete product to instantiate.

entries.title: Prototype pattern (create new objects by cloning an existing one)
entries.summary: Creates new objects by copying an existing instance through a clone operation, so the client produces objects without depending on their concrete classes.

entries.title: Singleton pattern (one shared instance, lazily and safely created)
entries.summary: Ensures a class has exactly one instance and gives a global access point to it, using Lazy<T> for thread-safe lazy initialization.

entries.title: The IDisposable / Dispose pattern (deterministic cleanup)
entries.summary: How to release unmanaged/expensive resources deterministically in C#: implement IDisposable, the protected Dispose(bool) override, the finalizer-only-when-you-own-unmanaged rule, and `using`. C# 5 compatible.

entries.title: Adapter pattern (wrap an incompatible type to match an expected interface)
entries.summary: Wraps an object with an incompatible interface in an adapter that exposes the interface a client expects, letting otherwise unusable classes work together.

entries.title: Bridge pattern (split an abstraction from its implementation so both vary independently)
entries.summary: Separates an abstraction (what) from its implementation (how) by composition so the two hierarchies can be extended independently instead of multiplying subclasses.

entries.title: Composite pattern (treat individual objects and groups of objects uniformly)
entries.summary: Builds a tree of objects where leaves and containers share one type, so client code can operate on a single item or a whole nested structure with the same call.

entries.title: Decorator pattern (add responsibilities to an object by wrapping it at runtime)
entries.summary: Wraps an object in one or more decorators that share its type and add behaviour, letting you layer responsibilities at runtime instead of subclassing every combination.

entries.title: Facade pattern (one simple entry point over a complex subsystem)
entries.summary: Provides a single high-level interface that orchestrates several subsystem classes, so clients call one method instead of wiring the subsystem themselves.

entries.title: Flyweight pattern (share immutable instances instead of allocating duplicates)
entries.summary: Caches and reuses shared instances through a factory so that many logical objects of the same kind are backed by one physical object, cutting allocation.

entries.title: Proxy pattern (a stand-in that controls access to a real object)
entries.summary: Puts a placeholder in front of a real object that shares its interface and controls access to it, here deferring the expensive real object's creation until first use (virtual proxy).

entries.title: Generate, compile, repair until it builds (the loop flow)
entries.summary: How the host turns a weak local model into a reliable code generator: a loop flow that generates C#, compiles it with the csc_check oracle, and feeds the compiler errors back into the next attempt until exit 0.

entries.title: Patch bytes at an offset, with a backup
entries.summary: Overwrite bytes at a known offset in a binary file after copying the original to a .bak - the safe write half of binary editing.

entries.title: Scan a file for a byte pattern (all offsets)
entries.summary: Read a file as bytes and find every offset where a byte pattern occurs, plus a hex-string parser - the basis for locating data in a binary asset or save file.

entries.title: Enumerate a running process's loaded modules
entries.summary: List the modules (DLLs/exe) a running process has loaded and their base addresses using System.Diagnostics.Process - the managed starting point for inspecting a target process.

entries.title: Drive an external CLI tool and capture its output
entries.summary: Run an external executable (dumpbin, sigcheck, a Sysinternals tool) with arguments, capture stdout/stderr without deadlocking, and enforce a timeout - the pattern the host's own ToolRunner uses.

entries.title: Minimal console app (csc -t:exe)
entries.summary: A ready-to-fill console app with a static Main built by the in-box csc as -t:exe, plus a build.ps1 (modeled on the host's) that globs *.cs and pins -langversion:5.

entries.title: Class library (csc -t:library)
entries.summary: A ready-to-fill class library (no Main) compiled to a DLL with the in-box csc as -t:library, with a public type and the exact reference line.

entries.title: P/Invoke wrapper class (DllImport + managed wrapper)
entries.summary: A P/Invoke wrapper: a private [DllImport] native declaration plus a clean public managed method, shown with a harmless kernel32 Beep and a SafeHandle-based file-handle example with proper release.

entries.title: Minimal WinForms app (csc -t:winexe)
entries.summary: A single-window WinForms app built with the in-box csc as -t:winexe: [STAThread] Main with EnableVisualStyles and Application.Run, a Form subclass with one control wired entirely in code (no designer).

entries.title: Advanced function template (CmdletBinding + pipeline)
entries.summary: A PS 5.1 advanced function template: [CmdletBinding()], typed and validated params, pipeline input via a process block, and begin/process/end - no ternary, no ??, no chain operators.

entries.title: Host tool (ToolRunner stdin contract)
entries.summary: A PowerShell instance-tool skeleton fitting this host's ToolRunner contract: read raw stdin bytes, strip a leading UTF-8 BOM, UTF-8 decode, optionally strip a markdown code fence, do the work, exit 0 on success / non-zero on failure.

entries.title: PowerShell module (.psm1 + .psd1 manifest)
entries.summary: A .psm1 module skeleton with one exported advanced function plus a matching .psd1 manifest skeleton, both PS 5.1 and both parse clean.
